title: The Longevity Clinic Is Coming for Your Members. Here's How to Not Lose Them.
deck: Next Health is nearing 20 locations. When health optimization becomes infrastructure, boutique fitness studios either plug in or get bypassed.

title: Lululemon Built a $10B Brand Without Ever Running a Single Ad. Here's What Fitness Studios Can Learn.
deck: No TV. No print. No paid social. Just obsessive product focus and local community ambassadors. The playbook is free — and most studios aren't using it.

title: Whoop Just Won a Fight Apple and Samsung Couldn't
deck: The FDA cleared Whoop's blood pressure feature — without the clearance Apple Watch has been chasing for years. Here's what it means for your studio.

title: The Vibe Era Is Over. What Comes Next for Pilates, Boxing, and Barre.
deck: Format fitness built an industry on atmosphere, community, and the feeling a class gives you. The studios that thrive in the next decade will be the ones with the clearest answer to a harder question: what results did your members get?

title: Exercise Is the Best Dementia Prevention Tool We Have. Fitness Studios That Miss This Miss a Generation.
deck: New research confirms regular exercise measurably slows cognitive decline. The opportunity for operators isn't just a wellness positioning shift — it's a referral network, a new pricing tier, and a program design question your competitors haven't asked yet.

title: Apple Is Building a Women's Health OS. Your Gym Is Either a Node in It or Invisible.
deck: iOS 27's perimenopause tracking and Apple Health+ aren't just features. They're Apple staking a claim on the 40+ female health journey — the same journey your millennial members are now on.

title: Daxko Just Bought an API Stack. Here's Why That Matters More Than the Acquisition.
deck: Most fitness software acquisitions lead with customer count. Daxko led with architecture. Here's what it means for operators on legacy platforms.

title: Planet Fitness Just Deployed AI Coaches. Here's What It Means for Independent Studios.
deck: When the 2,500-location chain moves, the whole market shifts. Independent operators have a narrow window to counter-position before AI coaching becomes the baseline.

title: 57% of Fitness Studio Members Churn in Year One. Most Operators Don't Know Why.
deck: The data is damning — and it's not about price, location, or equipment. Members who leave tell a consistent story. The question is whether you're listening before they go.

title: 80% of New Members Come From 3 Sources. Most Studios Are Funding the Other 20.
deck: Acquisition budgets get spread thin across every channel just in case. The data shows most studios are over-investing in low-yield channels while the three that actually drive new members stay underfunded.

title: You Think You Know Where Your Members Come From. You Don't.
deck: Most fitness studios credit referrals and Instagram — but have no data to back it up. The attribution gap costs more than most operators realize, and fixing it starts with three questions on a signup form.
